expectations.text: Identifies that t3 conflicts with both t1 and t2 since whole-file claim includes all sections
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 't1 vs t3: conflict — t3's file-level write overlaps t1's section write' and 't2 vs t3: conflict — t3's file-level write overlaps t2's section write'

expectations.text: Recognizes that t1 and t2 do NOT conflict since they write different sections
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 't1 vs t2: no conflict — different sections of the same file, safe to parallelize'

expectations.text: Places t1 and t2 together in one wave as parallel
expectations.evidence: Result shows 'Wave 1: t1, t2 (parallel — non-overlapping sections)'

expectations.text: Moves t3 to a separate wave from t1 and t2
expectations.evidence: Result shows 'Wave 2: t3 (solo — full-file write conflicts with wave 1)'

Resolution section explains: 'A full-file write (config.yml) supersedes any section-level write (config.yml#header, config.yml#footer). t3 cannot share a wave with t1 or t2.' Additionally, the table distinguishes section vs file scope, and the deeper issue section elaborates on why full-file writes coexisting with section writes is a design smell.
